title: tax — PHP attribute contracts
description: Two class-level attributes. `#[AsTaxProvider]` marks a class as a provider driver — the TaxProviderManager discovers these at boot and registers them via createXxxDriver factories. `#[AsExemptionVerifier]` marks a class as a per-jurisdiction exemption verifier (public-registry cross-check for certificate validity).

attributes.name: AsTaxProvider
attributes.description: Marks a class as a tax provider driver. The TaxProviderManager discovers these at boot and registers a createXxxDriver factory keyed on the slug. Every driver MUST implement the ITaxProvider contract (name(), supports(TaxJurisdiction), calculate(source_amount_cents, source_currency, jurisdiction, exemptions, applies_to), syncRates()) + ship a JSON Schema under data/providers/<slug>-config.schema.json for config validation.

params.name: slug
params.description: Provider slug matching the TaxProvider enum (taxjar, avalara, stripe_tax, native_calculator, custom_webhook). One driver class per slug.

params.name: supports_jurisdictions
params.description: Country codes (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) or 'ANY' the driver can calculate for. TaxJar: ['US', 'CA', 'AU', 'EU_ALL']. Avalara: ['ANY']. Stripe Tax: ['ANY']. NativeCalculator: ['ANY'] (works for any jurisdiction with rates in tax_rates). CustomWebhook: ['ANY'] (tenant-defined).

params.name: supports_exemptions
params.description: true = the driver honors exemption certificates in its calculation logic. NativeCalculator: true. Stripe Tax: false (Stripe manages exemptions internally via tax_exempt customer flag). TaxJar / Avalara / CustomWebhook: true.

params.name: supports_negative_amounts
params.description: true = the driver handles negative source_amount_cents (refund tax reversal). Every driver day-1 supports this.

params.name: requires_network
params.description: true = the driver dispatches HTTP requests + is subject to circuit-breaker + retry. false = NativeCalculator (local computation only, no network dependency).

attributes.name: AsExemptionVerifier
attributes.description: Marks a class as an exemption verifier for a specific (issuing_jurisdiction, exemption_type) pair. Every verifier cross-checks the certificate_number against the issuing jurisdiction's public registry (when one exists). VerifyTaxExemptionJob invokes these at exemption create.

params.name: country_code
params.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code the verifier handles (e.g. 'US', 'GB', 'DE').

params.name: exemption_type
params.description: TaxExemptionType value this verifier handles (reseller, non_profit, diplomatic, ...).

params.name: registry_endpoint
params.description: Public registry URL for cross-check. null = no public registry (verifier falls back to manual review by a tenant admin).
